codeReview.summary: The fix successfully adds and registers dedicated VAL-TYPES-004 unit suites for createProfile, resolveProfile, connection extraction, and storage-compatibility behavior, so the original missing-test-evidence blocker is resolved. The rerun still fails overall because the underlying shared-base-type mismatch from `shared-consolidate-profile-types` is unchanged, and the fix also introduces new scope/guardrail violations (two over-limit test files and bundled version bumps).

issues.description: The original blocking issue remains: `ProviderInstanceConnection` still only extends `Partial<ProviderConnectionConfig>`, and `ProviderInstance` still keeps a separate `providerType` field at lines 41-47 instead of sharing the extracted base shape directly. The new compatibility test only compares the extracted outputs (`tests/unit/suites/profile/compatibility.test.ts:94-122`), so the fix does not actually satisfy the original feature expectation that `ProfileConfig` and `ProviderInstance` share the base type.

This fix was supposed to stay within the mission's 200-line hard limit, but `tests/unit/suites/profile/core.test.ts` is 240 lines and `tests/unit/suites/profile/connection.test.ts` is 238 lines (`wc -l` on the added files). The handoff even claims the suites were split to meet line limits, so the delivered fix is still out of policy.

Commit `75fbc10` bundles unrelated version bumps in `package.json`, `packages/extension/manifest.json`, and `packages/extension/manifest.firefox.json` even though this feature only adds profile tests. Mission AGENTS explicitly forbids package/manifest version bumps in non-release refactor features, so the rerun widens scope beyond the assigned fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill still lacks a step that tells workers to verify the final diff is feature-scoped and free of forbidden version/manifest bumps. This fix handoff reports `followedProcedure: true`, yet commit `75fbc10` still bundled exactly the version changes that mission AGENTS forbids.

Shared-state docs are still inconsistent about the shared package layout. `shared-foundation-notes.md` correctly says provider types now live in `profile.ts`, `connection-config.ts`, and `provider-instance.ts`, but `architecture.md` still points workers to the removed `providers.ts` layout.

summary: Re-review failed. The fix does add the missing profile unit-test evidence and registers it in the unit runner, but it does not resolve the original shared-base-type blocker from `shared-consolidate-profile-types`. It also introduces two new blocking review issues: both major new test files exceed the mission's 200-line limit, and the fix commit again bundles forbidden package/manifest version bumps.
